macro_segments.title: Sharecropping with Crop Change: Liability for Breach, Profit-Sharing, and Land Depletion

macro_segments.title: Iska (Silent Partnership): Half-Loan, Half-Deposit Structure and Conditional Obligations

micro_segments.title: The Iska Institution: Half-Loan, Half-Pikadon Structure for Business Investment
Why Iska Works: Lender Gets Profit Share; Borrower Gets Capital and Retains Control
Nehardai's Position: Since Half Is a Loan, Borrower Can Use It for Personal Needs
Rava's Counter: Iska Requires Active Business Use; Loan Half Cannot Be Diverted
Rav Idi bar Avin: If Borrower Dies, Lender Cannot Claim Chattel Back from Heirs
Rava's Objection: Iska Contemplates Active Use and Sale; Lender Relies on Goods Remaining Collectible
Rashi's Explanation: Lender's Reliance on Specific Goods Overrides Normal Chattel-Collection Rules
